query_normalized: automated detection of algorithmic bias in public code repositories computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) and (b) because it explicitly identifies "bias" as a problem arising from software bots in open-source repositories and provides a dataset and detection methodology for bot behavior in the exact domain (public code repositories) specified by the user. While the paper focuses on detecting the *source* of bias (bots) rather than the bias itself, it establishes the necessary empirical baseline and mechanism for studying algorithmic bias in this specific context.
